mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn increases production costs and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which in turn reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary cycle, which in turn increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures (CPIAUCSL) signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 1-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening supply chain and labor market, which in turn reduces production costs and boosts consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply and labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
